message:
refactor(mcp): split ManageDependenciesTool add/remove into pre-validation + with_mutation

Inline the previous `dep_add` / `dep_remove` helpers into the
ManageDependenciesTool::call match arms and split each action into
two phases:

  1. Pre-validation under a *read-only* storage open
     (`open(&self.0)?`):
       - validate the source `id` (require_valid_issue)
       - for "add": validate the target id, parse the dep_type, and
         check for cycles (would_create_cycle); emit the existing
         CYCLE_DETECTED structured error if so
       - for "remove": placeholder check on `depends_on`
     None of these acquire the cross-process write lock.

  2. Mutation under `with_mutation(...)`, which actually takes the
     write lock and runs the storage write
     (`add_dependency` / `remove_dependency`).

Why split it: the previous shape ran the cycle probe + validation
inside the same `with_mutation` closure as the write, so every
"add" attempt — including the rejected-by-cycle ones — held the
cross-process write lock. Under contention with other agents
working in the same beads dir, that turned read-only validation
into a serialization point. Splitting the phases means we only
take the write lock when we're actually about to write.

Output shape, error codes, coercion handling, and the
suggested_tool_calls payload are all unchanged. The two helper
functions are no longer reused anywhere else, so they're dropped
in favour of the inlined match arms (no behaviour change at the
public tool boundary).

Cargo.lock left modified intentionally — same local-sibling-path
patch override situation flagged in the previous beads_rust pass;
committing it would revert chore(deps): regenerate Cargo.lock with
registry source/checksum for fsqlite family (068b793).
